mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate below 0.5% signals monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-08-15 indicates ultra-low rate environment
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals sustained labor market strength and consumer income expansion, which drives retail sales and equity valuations upward over a 60-day forward horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144,598 thousand as of 2016-08-15 indicates near-full employment
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth exceeds 150k, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals broad-based demand expansion across consumer and construction sectors, which compresses equity risk premiums and produces positive 20-day forward returns when both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS show positive month-over-month growth in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,211k and RSAFS at 456,321M as of 2016-07-15 indicate moderate demand
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of direction_20d on HOUST and RSAFS growth, or joint signal produces positive returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression in the manufacturing sector, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 52% of instances where INDPRO declines month-over-month, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer confidence, which dampens equity valuations and produces negative 60-day forward returns as investors anticipate slower earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.9% as of 2016-08-15 is near current threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year combined with elevated federal funds rate signals monetary tightening cycle that increases equity discount rates and reduces forward returns over 5 trading days as market reprices rate expectations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5% AND FEDFUNDS > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.828 and FEDFUNDS at 0.4% as of 2016-07-15
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 52% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly at rates above 2.0% annualized signals sustained economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and equity valuations, producing positive 60-day forward returns as investors maintain risk-on positioning.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 16,570.2 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2016-04-15
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during positive GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1,100 thousand units annualized signals weakening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic weakness, producing negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors anticipate slower GDP growth and reduced employment in construction-related sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,211 thousand as of 2016-07-15 is near threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand contraction
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where HOUST < 1,100k, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
